docs.title: UNLV MR Imaging Core (UMIC)
docs.text:
UNLV's hub for advanced brain imaging and interdisciplinary research using MRI technology

Welcome to the UNLV MR Imaging Core documentation site. This site serves as the central hub for researchers seeking access to UNLV’s MRI. Whether you are a new or returning user, this resource is designed to help ensure safe, compliant, and successful use of the UNLV MRI.

docs.title: MRI Prisma 3T
docs.text: We are proud to host the Siemens Prisma 3T MRI at UNLV.

docs.title: Location
docs.text:
Facility Location

Science and Engineering Building (SEB), Room 1235

docs.title: Facility Layout
docs.text:
• 1235A – Changing Room
  • 1235B – Gowning
  • 1235C – Waiting Lobby
  • 1235F – Scan Room
  • 1235G – Control Room

Facility map coming soon.

docs.title: Location
docs.text: • Zone III (Control Room)

docs.title: Critical Safety Warning
docs.text: The AED is NOT MRI Safe

docs.title: Proper Use
docs.text:
• Remove patient from Zone IV (magnet room)

  • Move to Control Room or Hallway

-Then apply AED

docs.title: Location
docs.text: • Zone II (Hallway / Changing Area)

docs.title: Important Safety Note
docs.text:
Only use fire extinguishers labeled MR SAFE

⚠️ Never bring a standard fire extinguisher into the magnet room (Zone IV)

docs.title: When should I use the electrical power emergency shutdown button?
docs.text:
Use the emergency power shutdown button when:

  • There is a fire in or near the MRI room

  • There is damage to the scanner that can be prevented by shutting down power

Note. Shutting down electrical power does NOT remove the magnetic field.

docs.title: Magnet Quench
docs.text: A quench collapses the magnetic field by releasing liquid helium.

docs.title: Only perform a quench if:
docs.text:
• A person is trapped by a metallic object

  • There is immediate life-threatening danger

docs.title: Do NOT quench for:
docs.text:
• Fire

  • Medical emergencies

  • Equipment issues

A quench is permanent and extremely costly.

docs.title: Patient Removal
docs.text: If a patient experiences a medical emergency inside the scanner:

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
• Locate the manual release button on the MRI table
  • Press and hold to disengage the motor
  • Pull the table out of the bore
  • Move the patient immediately out of Zone IV
  • Transport to designated medical response area

docs.title: Safety
docs.text:
Safety is the highest priority at the UNLV MR Imaging Core.

The MRI magnet is always active, and strict procedures must be followed to ensure the safety of all participants, staff, and equipment.

Use the sections in this menu to review specific safety procedures and emergency protocols.

docs.title: Screening Process
docs.text:
Safety Requirement

The MRI magnet is always on. Strict screening procedures must be followed at all times.

docs.title: 1. Pre-Screening
docs.text:
Participants must complete an MRI Screening Form.

Checks include:

  • Pacemakers or implants

  • Surgical clips or metal

  • Pregnancy or eye metal exposure

docs.title: 2. Physical Preparation
docs.text:
• Remove all personal items

  • Change into provided gown

  • Store belongings in lockers

docs.title: 3. Metal Detector
docs.text:
• Walk through detector slowly

  • If alarm sounds → return and remove metal

  • Entry not allowed until cleared

docs.title: 4. Technician Verification
docs.text: Final approval is given by a Level 3 MRI Technologist.

docs.title: Shutdown
docs.text:
# Shutdown \& Reboot

Instructions for safely shutting down and rebooting the MRI system will be provided here.

docs.title: Safety Training
docs.text:
Required Training

All personnel must complete safety training via CHIMERA before entering UMIC.

docs.title: Step 1: Access CHIMERA
docs.text:
• UNLV Students/Staff: Log in with your ACE credentials

  • External Users: Register for a CHIMERA account and complete MRI Safety Online Training

docs.title: Step 2: Access Levels
docs.text: Access is granted in three tiers:

docs.title: Non-MR Personnel
docs.text:
• Complete online module
  • Pass quiz (≥ 90%)

docs.title: Level 1
docs.text:
• Complete online module
  • Pass quiz (≥ 90%)

docs.title: Level 2
docs.text:
• Pass Level 1 exam
  • Complete 10+ hours supervised training
  • Final approval from Level 3 MRI Technologist

docs.title: Annual Recertification
docs.text: All certifications are valid for 1 year and must be renewed annually.
